# LAFS Documentation Index for LLM Agents

## Canonical entry points (use these first)

- `README.md` - Package overview and supported exports
- `docs/README.md` - Documentation landing page
- `docs/getting-started/quickstart.md` - Minimal working TypeScript path
- `docs/guides/llm-agent-guide.md` - Agent parsing + compliance patterns
- `docs/guides/schema-extension.md` - How to add custom message/result validation
- `docs/guides/compliance-pipeline.md` - Outgoing compliance middleware pattern

## Normative and code-truth sources

- `schemas/v1/envelope.schema.json` - Canonical JSON Schema
- `schemas/v1/error-registry.json` - Registered error codes
- `schemas/v1/conformance-profiles.json` - Machine-readable Core/Standard/Complete profiles
- `src/envelope.ts` - Envelope construction and parsing helpers
- `src/compliance.ts` - First-class compliance and middleware APIs
- `src/validateEnvelope.ts` - Runtime schema validator behavior
- `src/conformance.ts` - Conformance check semantics
- `src/flagSemantics.ts` - JSON/human format resolution and conflict error
- `src/mcpAdapter.ts` - MCP result wrapping behavior
- `src/index.ts` - Actual package export surface

## Questions this doc set answers directly

1. Extend validation for custom message types/fields:
   - `docs/guides/schema-extension.md`
2. Integrate validation into LLM generation pipeline:
   - `docs/guides/compliance-pipeline.md`
3. Enforce default mandatory JSON formatting policy:
   - `docs/guides/llm-agent-guide.md`
   - `src/flagSemantics.ts`

## Core envelope APIs

- `createEnvelope(input)`
- `parseLafsResponse(input, options?)`
- `LafsError`
- `validateEnvelope(input)`
- `runEnvelopeConformance(envelope)`
- `enforceCompliance(input, options?)`
- `assertCompliance(input, options?)`
- `withCompliance(producer, options?)`
